rawContent: {"stage":"build","insights":[{"kind":"convention","text":"Auth is enforced via `withAuth` HOC, not inline checks. Always use the HOC at the route level.","scope":"src/app/api/"},{"kind":"gotcha","text":"Landing page (`page.tsx`) may only do client-side auth checks without role enforcement — acceptable for public-facing entry points.","scope":"src/app/"},{"kind":"gotcha","text":"Frontend pages under `notes/*` may lack auth redirect UX even when API routes are protected; protect both layers independently.","scope":"src/app/notes/"},{"kind":"lesson","text":"Role enforcement (`withAuth({ roles: [...] })`) must be explicitly specified per route for instructor/admin pages — no inherited roles.","scope":"auth"},{"kind":"decision","text":"Two routes are intentionally public: `/health` and `/csrf-token` — these are explicitly excluded from auth coverage audits.","scope":"auth"}]}
